questions.question: Capstone использует Datasets для DAG triggering вместо cron. Когда event-driven превосходит cron?

options.text: (1) Variable upstream frequency (CDC, file uploads) — cron либо wastes runs либо lags; (2) Multi-source dependencies — все 3 Kafka topics обновлены trigger DAG; (3) Loose coupling между producer/consumer DAGs; (4) Audit trail (dataset events captured)

questions.explanation: Event-driven (Datasets/Assets) — natural fit для CDC sources, multi-source dependencies, loose coupling. Cron lacks: handle variable frequency (wastes empty runs), express multi-dataset triggers. Datasets capture events в DB — audit trail для compliance. Trade-offs: cron имеет predictable timing (good для SLA), simpler debugging. Hybrid pattern: cron-scheduled DAG produces datasets, consumers trigger from datasets — best of both.

questions.question: Capstone использует Dynamic Task Mapping (consume_kafka.expand(topic=[...])) — что это даёт?

options.text: Один task definition mapped across N inputs at runtime. 3 topics → 3 separate TIs автоматически. Если topics grow до 10, mapping scales без code change. Alternative — duplicate code per topic = unmaintainable

questions.explanation: Dynamic Task Mapping (AIP-42, 2.3+) — runtime task multiplication. Один decorator definition, при run-time scheduler creates N TIs (one per mapped value). Scales linearly without code duplication. Capstone usage: 3 Kafka topics today, easy add 10 tomorrow. UI shows mapped tasks как single graph node с count. Different from TaskGroup (UI grouping) — Mapping = dynamic instantiation.

questions.question: Multiple Executors (AIP-61, 2.10+) — light tasks на Celery, heavy на K8s. Зачем?

options.text: Resource optimization. Light tasks (consume_kafka, notify): Celery — fast scheduling, persistent worker pool. Heavy (spark_transform): K8s — own pod 4 CPU/8 GB, isolated, ephemeral. Without mixed: либо все на Celery (workers OOM от spark) либо все на K8s (10s overhead per consume_kafka — slow). 2026 sweet spot для mixed workloads

questions.explanation: AIP-61 enables single Airflow с multiple executors. Capstone use case ideal: light tasks frequent + short → Celery persistent workers, heavy Spark submit → K8s pod isolation + custom resources. Without mixed approach — compromise: Celery only fails on heavy tasks, K8s only слишком expensive overhead для light tasks. Migration к 3.x preserves этот pattern, may add Edge Executor (AIP-69).

questions.question: Capstone designed для smooth 3.x migration — most changes будут automatic через ruff AIR301/AIR302 fixes.
questions.explanation: Capstone uses modern patterns: TaskFlow @task decorators (auto AIR301 fix imports), Datasets (semantic identical к Assets в 3.x), standalone DAG Processor (3.x mandatory — already enabled), no SubDAGs, no Flask-AppBuilder direct integration, Multiple Executors (preserved в 3.x). Это intentional design — capstone serves как template для production DAGs ready для 3.x migration. Effort estimate: low (1-2 weeks для full migration codebase).
